mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or pinned near zero while CPIAUCSL is stable, real discount rates compress and equity valuation multiples expand. That setting predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of windows where FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 3 months and CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and lowers near-term equity prices. The effect should be negative over the next 20 trading days after the rise is observed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases or if the average 20-day return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rebound in HOUST and RSAFS indicates that housing activity and retail demand are recovering together, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support S&P 500 gains over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal from HOUST and RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of periods with concurrent 3-month improvements in both series.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising from recessionary lows, firms are restoring output and margins are stabilizing, which tends to produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal weakens once production is already back above trend.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 6-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if triggered rebounds in INDPRO fail to yield above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative once INDPRO has already recovered above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly surprise in GDPC1 relative to the prior quarter implies stronger aggregate demand and revenue growth, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest when unemployment is not rising at the same time.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth turns positive after a negative quarter
regime_hint: macro recovery with stabilizing labor market
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth does not predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt increase in FEDFUNDS after a low-rate period tightens financial conditions and raises discount rates, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is most direct when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25 percentage points within 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative after tightening episodes, or if the post-trigger average 5-day return is indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL is stable.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in PAYEMS and UNRATE indicates that payroll growth is weakening while unemployment is rising, a classic earnings-recession setup that should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not forecast negative 20-day returns, or if periods with payroll declines and rising unemployment still generate positive SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST remains depressed while RSAFS is also weak, housing-linked consumption and retail demand are both constrained, signaling broad demand softness that should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST stays below its prior 6-month average and RSAFS is flat or declining for 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weakness condition does not coincide with below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when only one of the two series is weak.
